target.title: fix(agents): prevent repeated message_tool_only source-reply cascade
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

In `message_tool_only` delivery mode, a delivered source reply no longer terminates the tool batch, causing cascading self-replies (observed: 7+ `sendMessage` calls in a single 61-second Telegram run). Add a `hasDelivered` closure flag that returns `terminate: true` only on subsequent deliveries, preserving the first delivery's ability to co-exist with follow-up tools.

- **Problem**: Commit `462092936a` (PR #92343) removed `return { ...hookResult, terminate: true }` from the hook to fix Slack follow-up tool continuation, but also removed the cascade guard.
- **Solution**: Track delivery count via a closure `hasDelivered` flag. First delivery passes through without terminating. Subsequent deliveries return `{ ...hookResult, terminate: true }`.
- **What changed**: 
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts` (+5 lines)
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts` (+380 lines: 3 new tests including 2 embedded-run proofs)
  - `packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts` (+148 lines: 2 agent-loop integration tests)
- **What did NOT change**: `isDeliveredMessageToolOnlySourceReply` detection; `onDeliveredSourceReply` callback; `attempt.ts` consumer; `shouldTerminateToolBatch` `every()` semantics

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #96827
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Evidence

### 1. Production function probe (`tools/proof-96827-prod.ts`)

```console
Call #1 (message.send, implicit, delivered):
  terminate: NO (expected: NO — first delivery)
Call #2 (message.send, cascade):
  terminate: YES ✓ (expected: YES — stop cascade)
Call #3 (message.send, isError=true):
  terminate: NO (expected: NO — error, hasDelivered unchanged)
Call #4 (message.send, after error recovery):
  terminate: YES ✓ (earlier delivery flag still set)
Call #5 (message.send, cascade after recovery):
  terminate: YES ✓
```

### 2. Unit tests (18 tests passing)

All edge cases: dry-run, error, explicit-route, suppressed-send, non-message-tool mode, cascade scenario, 5-turn lifecycle, error recovery.

### 3. Agent-loop integration tests (2 new tests in `agent-loop.test.ts`)

**Cascade**: Full agent-loop with `hasDelivered` pattern. Turn 1 sends → no terminate. Turn 2 sends → terminate. Loop stops at exactly 2 turns.

**Slack follow-up**: Turn 1 `message.send` → no terminate. Turn 2 `exec` runs. Turn 3 model stops. All 3 turns complete.

### 4. Full embedded-run proofs (2 new tests in `message-tool-terminal.test.ts`) ← ClawSweeper requested

These wire `installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook` through the real `agentLoop()` — exactly as `attempt.ts:2530` does — proving the entire chain works end-to-end:

```
installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook → agent.afterToolCall
  → agentLoop afterToolCall config
    → finalizeExecutedToolCall merge
      → shouldTerminateToolBatch every() contract
```

- **Cascade**: Turn 1 `message.send` → executes, no terminate. Turn 2 `message.send` → executes, terminate:true. Loop stops at exactly 2 turns, not 7+.
- **Slack follow-up**: Turn 1 `message.send` → no terminate. Turn 2 `exec` tool executes. Turn 3 model finishes. 3 turns, 2 tool executions, 1 delivery callback.

### Two-message cap rationale

The `afterToolCall` hook runs after `message.send` executes (per `finalizeExecutedToolCall` at `agent-loop.ts:977`). The second `message.send` is still delivered before `terminate: true` stops the batch — at most 2 visible replies instead of 7+. This is intentional:
- `beforeToolCall` only supports `{ block: true }` (prevents execution but doesn't terminate the batch)
- Preventing both the send AND terminating requires a new agent-core lifecycle hook — tracked as follow-up
- Reducing from 7+ to at most 2 is a significant improvement

## Risks

- **Mixed batches**: `message(send)` + `otherTool` where only the message terminates → batch continues under `every()` contract. Acceptable because the cascade scenario is single-tool-per-turn.
- **Compatibility**: Fully backward compatible. No config, API, or storage changes.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: claude-sonnet-4-6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: sunnyshu0925
